channel.title: Rob Burbea Guided Meditations
channel.description: A companion feed of Rob Burbea guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from Dharma Seed.
channel.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
channel.itunes:summary: A companion feed of Rob Burbea guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from Dharma Seed.

item.title: Metta Practice, and a few things about Piti (Instructions)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on using metta as a springboard into jhana, while clarifying how that purpose differs from cultivating metta for its own sake. He then turns to piti: what it is, how it can arise in different ways, and how to work with it once it is present through spread, narrow probing, and open receiving. The talk stays close to practice, with repeated emphasis on sensitivity, attunement, enjoyment, and not forcing the process.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the frame 00:57 Metta as springboard 04:56 Attunement and phrases 10:22 From metta to joy 11:51 What piti is 14:19 How piti arises 19:30 Let it pervade 21:04 Two modes of attention 25:07 Practice at the edge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60871/

item.title: Breathing with the Energy Body (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
This guided meditation explores the whole body as an energy body, using posture, breath, and imagination as mutually shaping supports for samadhi. Rob Burbea moves slowly through different points in the body—navel, solar plexus, heart, throat, crown, neck, back, and sacrum—while emphasizing sensitivity to tone, vibration, and what feels helpful. The talk closes with a short teaching section on how to hold these methods lightly and keep a clear sense of what is primary and what is secondary in practice.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 02:50 Whole-body awareness 05:38 Long breath and expansion 09:32 Breath at the navel 13:14 Moving through the midline 17:53 Heart and throat tour 23:46 Crown, neck, and back 34:07 Sacrum and release 37:20 Practice reflections
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60862/

item.title: The Energy Body and the Whole-body breath (Instructions and Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea begins with a short explanation of why this retreat will focus on whole-body breath and the energy body as supports for samadhi. He then leads a long guided meditation, moving from posture and whole-body awareness into the felt qualities of breath, including expansion, energization, relaxation, and subtle energy currents. In the closing talk, he returns to the same theme in practical terms: experiment rather than default, and let attention become lighter, more responsive, and more subtle both on and off the cushion.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the approach 02:41 Whole body and energy 05:06 A teaching story 12:04 Posture and presence 18:46 Breath in the whole body 27:05 Solar plexus play 34:02 What feels best 40:17 Review and principle 47:59 Daily life practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60884/

item.title: Counting Within the Breath (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation built around counting within the breath, moving through a longer count, then a shorter one, while keeping whole-body awareness open. After the practice, he explains why this method can steady attention, counter inertia, and support the conditions for jhana. He also gives practical retreat guidance on posture, movement, interviews, and how to work with comfort and energy.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 01:45 Counting Within Breath 29:27 Settling After Practice 30:31 Why It Helps 31:57 Attention and Jhana 33:48 Posture and Comfort 35:48 Movement and Energy 37:21 Interviews and Practice Focus 40:10 Practical Questions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60863/

item.title: Intention and Devotion (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea explores intention and devotion as ways of opening practice toward sacredness, moving from a contrast with renunciation into the more complex, generative terrain of imaginal practice. He then leads a guided exercise that uses standing, walking, and stillness to sense the body, heart, and energy body as part of a lived orientation toward the sacred. The talk closes with a quiet emphasis on humility, trust, and the need to keep returning to intention gently, like tending a flame.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Renunciation and desire 02:17 Imaginal complexity 05:49 The body becomes sacred 09:39 Movement and stillness 14:24 Intention toward sacredness 16:07 Standing exercise 21:36 Walking with holy fire 29:36 Returning to the flame 34:30 Bowing and closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/43942/

item.title: Eros in the Imaginal (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea begins by clarifying what he means by "imaginal": not merely visualization, but a way of perceiving that can involve the body, relationship, the emotional field, and a sense of mystery or sacredness. He then leads a long guided meditation that moves from settling the energy body into inviting an image, exploring how an imaginal figure relates, gazes, resonates, and sometimes becomes sexual or transfigures bodily assumptions. The talk ends by stepping back to explain how images may be discovered or deliberately chosen, and how energy body, emptiness, and ordinary life can all become entrances into imaginal practice.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 What Imaginal Means 10:01 Settling Into Practice 11:12 Energy Body and Samadhi 19:49 Letting An Image Form 23:41 Ways Of Relating 29:29 Eros And Transformation 37:19 Primary Sense And Resonance 48:25 Choosing And Receiving Images
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/43938/

item.title: Images of the Breath: Energy Body, Colour, and the Beloved (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation that moves from the felt energy of the body into several imaginal ways of breathing: through different centers, through the whole field, as light, and as color. The practice then opens into blessing and relationship, including the possibility of meeting an imaginal beloved and breathing in harmony with that figure. The talk ends by inviting listeners to notice how image changes the sense of self, others, and world.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Why images matter 03:06 Settling the body 06:51 Lower centers and grounding 11:23 Heart and crown 14:32 Front of body opening 20:08 Porous field of breath 23:51 Light and color 29:16 Blessing radiance 34:14 Meeting the beloved
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/37009/

item.title: Guided Meditation 1
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling posture and releasing obvious tension in the face, throat, shoulders, chest, and belly. It then widens into a larger field of awareness, first as a spacious bodily sense and then as a bright, energetic space that can be supported by imagined lines of energy and, optionally, the breath. The practice gradually becomes more imaginal, suggesting a radiant body of light and inviting the listener to notice what feels helpful rather than forcing any one visualization.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 01:18 Releasing tension 02:53 Grounding in contact 03:48 Opening the field 05:37 Restoring spacious presence 09:02 Imagining energy lines 11:43 Breath and support 15:18 Experimenting with form 19:00 Body of light
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/27488/

item.title: Body/Image - A Guided Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation that begins with grounding in the felt sense of the body and a gentle frame of kindness. The practice then moves through a detailed contemplative exploration of body parts and elements, shifting from anatomy into imagination, elemental reflection, and questions about solidity, space, and perception. In the final section, he introduces a luminous mind-made body as an optional imaginal exercise, then returns to stillness and integration.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 02:41 Settling In 03:32 Practice as Kindness 06:34 Exploring the Lower Body 16:39 Pelvis and Digestive System 24:23 Head and Nervous System 28:38 Elements and Interbeing 35:35 Solidity and Mystery 41:51 Luminous Body 51:00 Integration
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/14583/

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Seven)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a reflective wrap-up on working with emotions as one angle among many, emphasizing that there is no single best way to meet emotional life. He moves between seeing emotions as body sensations, as stories, as inner characters, and as fabrications shaped by self-view, while also noting that these views can be useful at different times. The talk ends by widening the frame: emotions can be healthy signals, traces of the past, or expressions of the psyche, and practice asks what each perspective opens up.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 One Perspective Among Many 01:19 Inner Dynamics and Characters 03:19 Stories and Simplicity 05:18 How Emotion Forms 07:42 What Fabrication Gives 10:56 Self-View and Looping 14:12 Courage to Feel 17:05 Resources Becoming Accessible
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13854/

item.title: Working With The Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Six)
item.description:
This guided meditation and teaching explores emotional practice through the simple move of allowing experience to be exactly as it is. Rob Burbea begins with posture, care, and a strong emphasis on kindness, then develops the idea that aversion and grasping add much of the suffering we feel. He goes on to connect this with anger, fear, the second arrow, and a deeper reflection on how relationship helps shape what we experience.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and kindness 03:21 Meeting what is prominent 08:17 Allowing as practice 15:15 Softening and letting go 24:45 Anger and fear 32:23 The second arrow 35:09 Relationship shapes experience 41:27 Letting be, not control 46:07 Doership and closing advice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13855/

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Four)
item.description:
Rob Burbea reviews the previous days of practice and then leads a guided meditation centered on posture, kindness, and widening awareness from the body into sound and space. The talk gradually shifts from intimate contact with experience to a much larger context, where thoughts, sensations, and even contraction can arise and pass without becoming a problem. In the closing discussion, he names fear, stillness, and the Buddha’s salt crystal metaphor to show how the felt weight of experience changes when it is held in a larger field.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Review and orientation 03:32 Posture and kindness 08:57 Centering the body 13:01 Opening to sound 19:35 Space and letting go 26:10 Including contraction 35:05 Discussion and salt crystal 43:45 Choosing the larger context
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13852/

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Three)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a compact set of ways to work with emotions in meditation by shifting attention through the body, noticing how labeling and interpretation shape experience, and using perception more intentionally. He moves from reviewing the earlier exercise to practical experiments with the emotional body, the mind's commentary, and simple supportive thoughts. The talk closes by encouraging a lighter, quicker relationship to experience, with kindness kept in view throughout.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Reviewing the approach 04:07 The energy body 06:33 Feeling and enjoyment 08:24 Mind's commentary 14:46 Short practice experiments 20:09 A supportive view 24:18 Unweighting the moment 28:53 Quickness and kindness
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13856/

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Two)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a set of practical instructions for working with tiredness, physical discomfort, and emotional difficulty in meditation. He emphasizes a patient, exploratory attention: softening aversion, broadening awareness, and noticing how experience changes moment by moment. The talk includes a guided practice and short reflections on how a more open field of attention can become a source of safety and resource.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Introducing the approach 07:03 Tiredness and aversion 09:58 Working with discomfort 13:54 Softness and detail 20:39 Cézanne and patience 24:15 Guided central line practice 33:51 Expanding the field 40:30 Inquiry and resourcing 50:51 Two practices to explore
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13857/

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day One)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers an introduction to working with emotions and mind states through the body, framing practice around the Pali term citta as heart-mind. He emphasizes not trying to get rid of feeling, but learning to meet it with interest, kindness, and enough attention that difficult states become workable rather than overwhelming. The talk moves from explanation into two guided practices, with instructions for sitting and walking meditation and a steady return to the body as the place where emotion can be known.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Heart-Mind Framing 04:33 What We Want 09:21 Mind States And Emotion 15:09 Helpful Attention 20:05 Body-Based Practice 29:22 Allowing And Exploring 40:19 Letting Go Of Demands 45:14 Second Sitting 54:51 Object And Walking Practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13853/

item.title: Guided Meditation - The Play of Appearances
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the posture and letting awareness pervade the body, then moves into a gentle practice of receiving whatever arises. From there, the instruction broadens toward welcoming thoughts, emotions, and self-images as appearances in space, without making them into a problem. The meditation closes by emphasizing release, the felt peace of letting go, and a quiet sense that all things belong to awareness.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the seat 02:15 Receiving and softening 06:18 Appearances in space 11:03 Welcoming selfing 15:54 Bowing to experience 20:27 Letting the heart release 26:24 Floating in space 32:47 Peace in all things
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12522/

item.title: Seventh Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation: Metta and Emptiness
item.description:
Rob Burbea begins by framing metta as a practice with six flexible categories, then turns to the move from a personal wish for goodwill toward an open, boundless love that can include all beings. He explores how spaciousness, perception, and samadhi change what feels like a problem, and how aversion quietly closes the heart in ordinary situations like tiredness, cold weather, or wanting comfort. The talk ends with practical encouragement to question habitual patterns gently and to keep the practice responsive rather than rigid.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Metta categories 02:53 Turning toward self 05:19 Methods for all beings 08:36 Space filled with love 13:20 Perception and emptiness 17:11 Samadhi and insight 19:16 Aversion closes the heart 24:11 Questioning habit 30:26 Return to practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12526/

item.title: Fifth Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea continues a metta retreat teaching by introducing the neutral person and exploring why this category can feel dull, spacious, or unexpectedly difficult. He reflects on shared humanity, vulnerability, mortality, and the balance between metta and compassion, then turns into a guided practice that first settles the body and then opens the heart toward a neutral person. The meditation emphasizes gentle persistence, flexible phrases, and letting care spread without forcing it.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Introducing the neutral person 03:26 Shared humanity 08:50 Phrases and flexibility 12:13 Stretching the heart 15:46 Self, friend, and shadow 18:12 Settling into practice 27:17 Neutral person meditation 39:41 Closing and return
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12515/

The meditation emphasizes gentle persistence, flexible phrases, and letting care spread without forcing it.

Chapters: 00:00 Introducing the neutral person 03:26 Shared humanity 08:50 Phrases and flexibility 12:13 Stretching the heart 15:46 Self, friend, and shadow 18:12 Settling into practice 27:17 Neutral person meditation 39:41 Closing and return
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12515/

item.title: Fourth Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a practical review of metta, emphasizing that there is no single right way to practice and that body, phrases, and imagery can all serve kindness. He then moves through a detailed guided meditation, widening awareness through the whole body and using a ray-of-light image to support uprightness, connectedness, and ease. The talk closes by returning to lightness, gentleness, and patience as the right tone for practice.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Reviewing the practice 08:17 Samadhi as spaciousness 12:59 Working with difficulty 23:01 Whole-body metta 28:23 Boundless in steps 30:09 Guided body meditation 41:05 Silent cultivation 50:24 Closing tone
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12519/

item.title: Second Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on metta as something responsive rather than rigid: sometimes steady, sometimes playful, sometimes set aside when difficulty becomes too strong. He distinguishes between ease, workable difficulty, and situations that call for a different kind of kindness altogether, then leads a long guided practice moving from posture and body awareness into self-metta and then metta for another person. The talk stays close to lived experience, with emphasis on warmth around difficulty, softening self-judgment, and trusting the varied textures of loving-kindness.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Responsive metta 01:29 The sailing analogy 04:47 Working with difficulty 10:30 Mindfulness with warmth 12:21 Phrases and resonance 19:02 Allowing well-being 26:23 Self and easiest person 33:23 Guided metta practice 48:29 Closing the practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12524/

item.title: First Instructions and Guided Metta Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided metta meditation that begins with posture, body awareness, and a sense of being held, then moves into phrases of self-kindness and a wider, more spacious field of attention. After the guided practice, he explains metta as unconditional well-wishing, and emphasizes flexibility: using phrases, visualization, or body-sense in whatever way is most supportive. He closes by encouraging patience, noting that even small moments of kindness accumulate over time, like planting seeds and trusting their own pace.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Entering the practice 04:32 Opening the field 10:11 Self-directed metta 19:10 Seeds and tenderness 26:23 The easiest person 34:04 Returning to openness 36:29 What metta means 40:10 Ways of practicing 48:04 Patience and walking
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12516/

item.title: Liberation and the Self - A Guided Inquiry
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided inquiry into liberation, freedom, and the self, beginning with a gentle explanation of how inquiry can uncover both hidden strengths and limiting beliefs. He then walks through a series of questions that invite listeners to examine what liberation means to them, how that idea affects their practice and life, and what self-view may be operating around it. The talk stays close to embodied experience throughout, emphasizing kindness, curiosity, and a willingness to trust whatever is actually present.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the inquiry 04:23 Inquiry as an art 11:43 How to work with it 15:49 First question: liberation 29:33 Second question: its effects 39:16 Third question: self-view 47:31 Letting go of locking
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11968/

item.title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 5
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers practical metta instructions that move from the body, to difficulty, to an increasingly boundless field of kindness. He emphasizes that the practice is hard to do “wrong,” and keeps returning to flexibility: comfort in the body, use of phrases or visualization, and kindness toward whatever is present. In the second half, the talk opens into the Metta Sutta, a discussion of how metta changes perception, and a long guided meditation that widens from a bodily sense of ease to all beings.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Metta is forgiving 02:43 Working with difficulty 04:37 Care for the body 09:03 Energy and imagination 14:14 The Metta Sutta 16:44 How metta changes perception 23:42 Guided practice begins 25:56 Opening to all beings 34:12 Seeds without pressure
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9987/

item.title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 4
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers metta as a practice of balance: not collapsing into suffering, but cultivating enough buoyancy, brightness, and steadiness to meet difficulty well. He moves from general guidance on how to approach a difficult person, into a guided meditation that begins with the easy places and gradually opens toward more complicated relationships. The talk closes with reminders about working skillfully with stories, returning to self-kindness, and carrying metta into everyday movement.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Balancing metta and compassion 02:18 Approaching difficult people 07:05 What to reflect on 09:58 Working with hurt and anger 11:44 Settling into meditation 25:13 Opening to a difficult person 31:02 Spacious, patient effort 39:14 Story and self-definition 43:21 Returning and carrying on
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9986/

item.title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 3
item.description:
Rob Burbea first speaks about why receiving kindness can feel harder than giving it, and suggests ways to notice support from nature, community, and one’s own metta practice. He then introduces the day’s new categories—the neutral person and the difficult person—offering reflections on common humanity, mortality, and the difference between metta and compassion before beginning a guided meditation. The meditation moves gently through posture, self-metta, the neutral person, and the closing return to the body.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Receiving Kindness 08:28 Metta And Energy 11:14 Neutral Person Reflections 18:30 Settling For Practice 21:26 Self Metta And Easiest Person 32:08 Neutral Person Practice 35:52 Closing And Return
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9985/

Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9985/

item.title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 2
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a detailed set of metta instructions, emphasizing a light touch: gentle effort, responsiveness, and flexibility rather than pressure. He walks through how to work with hindrances, how the phrases can be used in different ways, and how metta may show up as calm, warmth, brightness, compassion, or even no clear feeling at all. The guided practice then moves from body awareness to self or benefactor, and finally to a friend, with imaginative ways of supporting loving-kindness throughout.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Setting the tone 03:42 Working with hindrances 06:36 Flexible practice forms 10:16 What metta can feel like 13:58 Body and resonance 19:32 Simple self and basics 24:21 Using imagination 32:49 Metta for a friend 43:04 Closing the practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9984/

item.title: Metta Instructions and Guided Meditation 1
item.description:
Rob Burbea introduces a metta practice centered on self-kindness and the benefactor, with a strong emphasis on flexibility rather than formula. He explains how to work with phrases, imagery, and body-sense, and why the practice is more about planting intentions than manufacturing a feeling. The talk then moves into a guided meditation, first for oneself and then for a benefactor, with guidance on walking practice at the end.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Metta and Boundlessness 03:05 Finding the Benefactor 05:05 Playful Practice 08:27 Working with Phrases 11:46 Feeling and Intention 16:41 Self Metta Guided 23:56 Self Kindness Continues 33:23 Turning to the Benefactor 46:55 Imagery and Walking
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9983/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Vastness of Awareness
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and gathering attention, then gradually opens awareness to sound, silence, and the sense of spaciousness itself. Rob Burbea uses repeated imagery—open sky, ocean, waves, reflections, and fireflies—to invite a felt sense that appearances arise and fade within an unshakable field of awareness. The closing turns to how to practice this in walking, standing, and with eyes open or closed.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 01:19 Body and Stillness 04:11 Opening to Sound 05:50 Silence as Ground 09:57 Sky of Awareness 14:15 Letting Everything Belong 18:43 Waves and Reflections 24:31 Repeated Refrain 36:06 Pervading Practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9544/

Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9544/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Three Characteristics
item.description:
Rob Burbea leads a guided meditation on the three characteristics, beginning with settling the body and moving through impermanence, reactivity, and not-self. The practice shifts from narrow attention on sensations to a wider field that includes the body, sounds, and the visual field, then into a gentler way of letting experience be. In the closing review, he emphasizes balancing samadhi and insight and warns against turning practice into a search for special experiences.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In 04:43 Impermanence in the Body 10:09 Wider Field of Change 20:24 Reactivity and Dukkha 27:32 Letting Be 29:40 Not-Self Practice 39:00 Seeing Without Clinging 47:17 Balancing the Practice 53:16 Two Ways of Not-Self
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9543/

item.title: The Space of Awareness - Welcoming (4th Guided Meditation)
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea leads a steady practice of welcoming whatever is most present, beginning with posture, bodily balance, and a gentle aspiration toward kindness. The attention gradually widens from a single sensation or mood to the whole body, then to sounds, the full field of experience, and even the subtle sense of resistance itself. As the practice deepens, he emphasizes relaxing the tension of aversion and clinging, and resting in a spacious openness that can include blur, fading, and change.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 01:17 Setting kindness 02:37 Welcoming what arises 04:21 Opening the whole body 06:11 Including sounds and space 08:31 Resting in allowance 14:12 Returning to welcoming 27:57 Working with resistance 34:22 Welcoming subtlety
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9813/

item.title: The Space of Awareness - Only Impressions in Awareness (3rd Guided Meditation)
item.description:
This guided meditation moves from settling the body and remembering kindness into sensing stillness, silence, and spacious awareness. Rob Burbea then uses a series of imaginal analogies—sky, fireflies, waves, reflections, bubbles—to point to experience as appearing and fading within awareness, without needing to be grasped or controlled. The final section gently extends the practice into daily postures and situations, inviting exploration beyond the sitting meditation.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Opening the retreat field 00:42 Settling with kindness 02:29 Sensing stillness 04:33 Listening into silence 08:04 Sky of awareness 12:13 Nothing to hold 22:23 Impressions in awareness 31:53 Self as appearance 35:31 Qualities and aftercare
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9820/

item.title: The Space of Awareness - Silence (2nd Guided Meditation)
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea leads a gradual settling into the space of awareness through posture, kindness, and listening. The practice widens from bare sound to the whole field of hearing, then to body sensations, thoughts, and images, emphasizing openness, presence, and non-clinging. He repeatedly turns toward silence as something that can be sensed within and around sound, until everything is invited to rest in that spaciousness.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 01:54 Kindness and grounding 03:21 Listening to sound 05:21 Global sound field 09:02 Sounds arising and passing 12:17 Body in awareness 15:27 Everything is included 24:33 Silence within sound 30:20 Belonging to silence
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9817/

item.title: Breath Meditation (2nd Instructions/Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea leads a long guided breath meditation that begins with posture, kindness, and widening the field of awareness through the whole body. He then invites listeners to explore the breath from several centers—below the navel, the heart, throat, mid-head, crown, base of spine, upper back, and elsewhere—treating each as an experiment in what feels most open, comfortable, or enjoyable. The final section shifts into practical advice for working with pain and discomfort, emphasizing relaxation, spaciousness, imagination, and the freedom to change posture when needed.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and setup 00:26 Posture and kindness 02:10 Whole-body awareness 03:18 Lower belly and right side 09:34 Midline and heart 15:59 Head and crown centers 21:55 Base and upper back 29:05 Review and possibilities 39:23 Working with discomfort
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9818/

item.title: The Space of Awareness - Impermanence (1st Guided Meditation)
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Rob Burbea invites a spacious, open awareness and turns it toward impermanence: first in a single area of body sensation, then across the whole body, then into sounds, thoughts, and the full field of experience. The practice stays close to bare change—arising, passing, flickering—while encouraging an alert but relaxed attention that can support letting go. Near the end, he reflects on the wider teaching context and notes that this is one of two practices to be explored in different ways across the retreat.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the practice 02:14 Settling the body 05:06 Widening to the body 10:10 Including sound 14:11 Only the change 18:46 Working with difficulty 24:02 Everything in the field 28:56 Space and flicker 34:12 Letting go, closing
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9811/

Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9811/

item.title: Breath Meditation (1st Instructions and Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided breath meditation that begins with posture and bodily ease, then gradually widens into a whole-body awareness of breath, energy, and comfort. He repeatedly invites the listener to experiment gently with long or short breathing, to include subtle pleasure or ease when it is present, and to keep returning with patience when the mind wanders. Later in the talk he pauses the meditation to explain why this approach matters: it can build a quiet inner resource, support calm and brightness, and make insight more available.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Settling the posture 04:49 Kindness and body space 13:47 Long breath, whole body 20:50 Choosing what helps 30:12 Why practice this way 39:03 Six practical reminders 43:29 Patience and adjustment
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9812/

item.title: Guided Meditation - Cittamatra
item.description:
This guided meditation develops a quiet, spacious way of attending in which body, sound, silence, and thought are all received without interference. Rob Burbea repeatedly returns to the imagery of awareness as sky-like and imperturbable, then later reframes all experience—including the sense of self—as an impression in awareness. Near the end, he connects the practice to three characteristics work and offers a few practical ways to try it with eyes open or closed.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body 02:17 Body and stillness 05:51 Sound and silence 08:25 Awareness as sky 11:02 Letting things be 25:55 Impressions in awareness 36:27 Waves on the ocean 41:36 Three characteristics and practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11125/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
This guided meditation develops a quiet, spacious way of attending in which body, sound, silence, and thought are all received without interference. Rob Burbea repeatedly returns to the imagery of awareness as sky-like and imperturbable, then later reframes all experience—including the sense of self—as an impression in awareness. Near the end, he connects the practice to three characteristics work and offers a few practical ways to try it with eyes open or closed.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body 02:17 Body and stillness 05:51 Sound and silence 08:25 Awareness as sky 11:02 Letting things be 25:55 Impressions in awareness 36:27 Waves on the ocean 41:36 Three characteristics and practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11125/

item.title: Guided Meditation on the Three Characteristics
item.description:
This guided meditation introduces three ways of working with experience: attending closely to impermanence, softening aversion or grasping, and seeing sensations, sounds, and thoughts as not-self. The practice moves through the hands, face, whole body, sound, sight, and thought, with brief pauses to let the sense of change become clearer. In the closing section, Rob also speaks about the unfamiliarity that can arise as letting go deepens, and how to meet fear by inclining toward what is peaceful, spacious, or warm.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Three approaches 01:20 Settling the mind 05:54 Impermanence in the hands 11:40 Face and whole body 15:15 Sound and relation 26:39 Allowing experience 30:04 Not-self in awareness 40:13 Walking through the day 47:55 Fear and familiarization
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11124/

item.title: Second Morning Instructions and Guided Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a second-morning review of concentration practice, emphasizing whole-body awareness, sensitivity, steadiness, and working skillfully with discomfort. He then lays out a set of practical options for pain, restlessness, dullness, doubt, and craving, framing each as something to meet with experimentation rather than force. The talk closes with a long guided meditation that moves the breath through different points in the body, from the lower belly to the crown and feet, always returning to a whole-body field of awareness.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Reviewing the basics 02:29 Sensitivity and steadiness 06:05 Working with discomfort 08:40 Breath as a resource 11:18 Hindrances and discernment 16:32 Settling the posture 21:22 Breath through the body 31:28 Down the body and feet 40:28 Centering and spaciousness
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11512/

item.title: First Morning Instructions and Guided Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea gives a guided meditation that begins with posture, relaxing the face, throat, shoulders, and belly, then opens into wide whole-body awareness. He gradually works with the breath as a gentle way to energize, soothe, and calm the body, and then shifts into finding a place of comfort and letting it be nourished without pressure. Near the end, he recaps the method as an exploratory practice of patience, responsiveness, and contentment, with comfort allowed to grow slowly and spread through the body.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:01 Settling the posture 04:24 Whole-body awareness 11:36 Working with the breath 23:58 Breath as nourishment 36:40 Play without forcing 40:41 Comfort that can spread
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11510/

item.title: Guided Meditation - Directing Love Towards dharmas
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and opening attention to whatever is most prominent in experience. It then repeatedly invites that experience to be bathed, held, and totally welcomed in kindness and compassion, expanding the practice to include resistance, numbness, peace, happiness, and even the intention to be kind. The closing turns toward a quiet inquiry: whether anything exists independent of our relationship to it.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body 02:03 Meeting what stands out 06:27 Bathing experience in kindness 12:23 Letting precision soften 20:12 Nothing is exempt 26:04 Including self and intention 34:26 Relationship and inquiry
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11957/

item.title: Guided Meditation on Gratitude and Compassion
item.description:
This guided meditation moves from settling the body into a widening contemplation of gratitude and compassion. It begins with a benefactor, then expands through teachers, parents, friends, difficult people, strangers, and finally the whole web of human and more-than-human life. Rob Burbea closes by offering simple ways to practice compassion for all beings, including an option to let the body radiate compassion in space.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Body 01:48 Benefactor and Compassion 05:08 Many Who Have Helped 15:00 Parents and Early Care 19:06 Friends and Intimacy 23:04 Unknown Benefactors 29:35 Trade and Difficult People 36:28 Animals and Ecosystem 45:10 Practice Options and Close
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11952/

item.title: Third Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided samatha practice that moves the center of awareness through different places in the body, asking what kind of breath feels most easeful and nourishing at each point. He frames the practice as an experiment in sensitivity rather than a fixed method, and pauses afterward to explain how body-centered attention can support pleasure, steadiness, and whole-body awareness. In the Q&A, he also addresses difficult emotions, the spider-web image for centered awareness, and practical issues like a blocked nose.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Posture 01:24 Lower Belly Center 07:05 Right Side and Belly 14:19 Solar Plexus and Heart 18:49 Throat to Crown 28:32 Spine and Return 31:59 After the Sit 37:18 Questions and Context 46:08 Closing Instructions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12314/

item.title: Second Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers practical samatha instructions, moving from the long breath to finding the breath that is most comfortable, enjoyable, and supportive of collectedness. He emphasizes keeping the whole body in awareness, balancing energizing and calming, and working skillfully with hindrances, self-judgment, and the tendency to fixate on what is difficult. The talk then turns to discomfort in sitting, with several ways to work with pain before ending in a guided settling into posture and breath, followed later by a stretch of repeated literary and teacher references, especially Rainer Maria Rilke.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Gathering the instructions 05:25 Balancing energy and calm 08:39 Working with hindrances 10:53 Turning toward pleasantness 13:12 Meeting sitting pain 14:43 Posture and settling 17:03 Guided breath practice 27:27 Influences and recitations
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12313/

item.title: First Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation on the Breath
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers first instructions for samatha practice by placing breath meditation inside a larger Buddhist frame: cultivation on one side, insight on the other. He then guides a long practice that emphasizes patience, non-judgment, kindness, and the quality of attention over counting or achievement. The meditation moves through posture, relaxing the body, lengthening the breath, and widening awareness so the breath can feel bright, comfortable, and alive.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 The bigger picture 02:26 Why practice with breath 04:44 Patience over self-judgment 10:06 Quality, not counting 12:22 Settling into the body 25:38 Whole-body breathing 34:14 Adjusting the breath 39:29 Bright and alive
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12310/

item.title: Guided Meditation: Metta to phenomena
item.description:
This guided meditation turns lovingkindness and compassion toward experience itself rather than toward people. Rob Burbea invites repeated softening, bathing whatever is present in kindness, and then experiments with a more receptive mode of complete welcoming. The emphasis gradually shifts away from precision and analysis toward acceptance, including resistance, numbness, fear, anger, and tiredness.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Turning Toward Experience 01:28 Bathing the Moment 04:07 Holding with Metta 07:30 Open Reception 09:51 Softening Repeatedly 13:47 Less Precision, More Kindness 18:07 Welcoming All States 20:58 Long Receptive Silence
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12292/
